4. 
The accused filed a criminal revision petition before the High Court 
which came to be allowed vide the Impugned Order and the conviction 
and sentence imposed on the accused/respondent no.2 was set 
aside. While doing so, the High Court noted as under, inter alia:
‘The complainant has failed to prove his case that the cheque 
was issued towards discharge of a lawful debt specially when the 
complainant has failed to disclose details of his Bank Account 
and date when he withdrew the amount in question and paid to 
the revisionist as well as the date when he obtained the cheque. 
Therefore, there are glaring inconsistencies indicating doubt in the 
complainant’s version, hence, the conviction and sentence cannot 
be sustained.’
2
